episodes.title: The Current Below
episodes.description: An introduction to the deep ocean thermohaline circulation and why it regulates global climate.

episodes.title: Coral Cities
episodes.description: The richest reef ecosystems on Earth are also the most threatened — a look at what remains.

episodes.title: Migration Routes
episodes.description: Tracking the epic migrations of whale sharks, manta rays, and bluefin tuna via tagging data.

episodes.title: Darkness Dwellers
episodes.description: Life at depths below 2,000 meters — creatures adapted to crushing pressure and total darkness.

episodes.title: The Invisible Harvest
episodes.description: How industrial fishing intersects with deep ocean ecosystems most consumers never see.

episodes.title: Return Currents
episodes.description: The scientists working to measure, model, and predict the ocean's long-term changes.

episodes.title: New Surveys
episodes.description: A new generation of autonomous deep-sea vehicles maps previously unexplored trenches.

episodes.title: Plastic Depth
episodes.description: Microplastics have reached the hadal zone — the deepest point on Earth.

episodes.title: Temperature Record
episodes.description: Ocean heat content records are being broken — and the effects cascade upward.

episodes.title: The Nurseries
episodes.description: Deep water nursery areas for large pelagic species are being disrupted by trawling.

episodes.title: Restoration
episodes.description: Marine protected areas and the evidence on whether they're actually working.

episodes.title: Signal
episodes.description: Early warning systems — what the ocean is telling us about the decades ahead.

extras.title: Deep Dive Tech
extras.description: The submersibles and sensor platforms used to film in extreme conditions.

extras.title: Field Notes
extras.description: Narrated excerpts from the research team's expedition journals.
